title: Synapse → Cerebro migration bundle (v1)
description: Wire shape produced by Synapse's `migrate_export.export()` and consumed by Cerebro's `POST /v1/admin/migrate/import`. This file is the SINGLE source of truth for the migration contract — both repos validate against it.

format_version.description: Bundle format version. Cerebro rejects anything other than 1.

backend.description: Cerebro rejects bundles whose source_backend.backend is not 'synapse'.

counts.description: Per-resource row counts produced by the export. Informational; the importer trusts the array lengths.

councils.description: Synapse council session rows. Cerebro stores them under the destination tenant_id (the source tenant_id field is recorded in settings).

audit_events.description: Synapse audit-log rows. Currently accepted by Cerebro but not yet persisted (see Synapse.Migration moduledoc) — counted under `skipped`. The schema is intentionally permissive until the import path lands.

notification_prefs.description: Per-principal notification preferences exported via GET /v1/admin/notifications/preferences.

device_tokens.description: Per-principal device tokens exported via GET /v1/admin/notifications/devices.

api_keys.description: API key metadata exported via GET /v1/admin/api-keys. Raw key material is NOT included — Cerebro records these as a re-issuance manifest.

webhooks.description: Webhook registrations exported via GET /v1/admin/webhooks. Signing secrets are included for migration continuity.

threads.description: Council thread history (S-MIG-THREADS) exported via GET /v1/admin/threads. Each thread carries its full append-only event log under `events`. Currently accepted by Cerebro but not yet persisted — counted under `skipped` like audit_events. Decision pending: add a Thread/ThreadEvent schema to Cerebro vs collapse events into Council.chat_messages.

SynapseCouncil.description: One row from Synapse's GET /v1/councils. The importer reads `session_id` (preferred) or `id` as the source UUID and preserves it in Cerebro.

status.description: Synapse lifecycle status. The importer maps this to one of Cerebro's draft|running|closed via Synapse.Migration.map_status/1.

tenant_id.description: The Synapse-side tenant_id. Cerebro IGNORES this on import — councils land under the caller's JWT-claimed tenant. Recorded in settings.synapse_source_tenant for forensic recovery.

SynapseNotificationPrefs.description: One row from GET /v1/admin/notifications/preferences. The `id` UUID is preserved in Cerebro for idempotent re-imports.

SynapseDeviceToken.description: One row from GET /v1/admin/notifications/devices. The `id` UUID is preserved in Cerebro for idempotent re-imports.

SynapseApiKey.description: API key metadata from GET /v1/admin/api-keys. Raw key material is never stored; Cerebro records this for operator re-issuance tracking.

SynapseWebhook.description: One row from GET /v1/admin/webhooks. The `id` UUID is preserved in Cerebro for idempotent re-imports. The `secret` field is the plain HMAC signing key.

secret.description: Plain HMAC signing secret for migration continuity.

SynapseThread.description: One thread + its full event timeline from GET /v1/admin/threads. council_id is null for standalone chat threads (rare in practice — most threads are council-backed). Cerebro currently records but does not persist these; see the `threads` resource description.

SynapseThreadEvent.description: One event from a thread's append-only timeline. Synapse uses this for chat messages, stage progress, member responses, ranking summaries, verdicts, reflections, precedent hits, and system events — see ThreadEventType.
